heading: Mass mailing sent by House Members
label: 506 Mass mailing sent by House Members
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 59h of this titleSection is from the Legislative Branch Appropriations Act, 1997.

Amendments2020—Subsec. (a). designated existing provisions as par. (1), substituted “one of the notices described in paragraph (2) or a notice” for “the following notice: “this mailing was prepared, published, and mailed at taxpayer expense.”, or a notice”, and added par. (2).Pub. L. 116–260

children.heading: Notice that mailing is at taxpayer expense
children.label: a Notice that mailing is at taxpayer expense

children.text: Each mass mailing sent by a Member of the House of Representatives shall bear in a prominent place on its face, or on the envelope or outside cover or wrapper in which the mail matter is sent, one of the notices described in paragraph (2) or a notice to the same effect in words which may be prescribed under subsection (c). The notice shall be printed in a type size not smaller than 7-point.

children.label: A
children.text: “Paid for with official funds from the office of _____.”, with the blank filled in with the name of the Member sending the mailing.

children.label: B
children.text: “Paid for by the funds authorized by the House of Representatives for District __ of _____.”, with the first blank filled in with the name of the congressional district number, and the second blank filled in with the name of the State, of the Member sending the mailing.

children.label: C
children.text: “Paid for by official funds authorized by the House of Representatives.”

children.heading: Publication of each Member’s total expense and amount
children.label: b Publication of each Member’s total expense and amount

children.text: There shall be published in the itemized report of disbursements of the House of Representatives as required by law, a summary tabulation setting forth, for the office of each Member of the House of Representatives, the total number of pieces of mass mail mailed during the period involved and the total cost of those mass mailings.

children.label: A
children.text: the total cost (as referred to in paragraph (1)) divided by the number (as determined by the Postmaster General) of addresses (other than business possible delivery stops) in the Congressional district from which the Member was elected (as such addresses are described in ); andsection 3210(d)(7)(B) of title 39

children.label: B
children.text: the total number of pieces of mass mail (as referred to in paragraph (1)) divided by the number (as determined by the Postmaster General) of addresses (other than business possible delivery stops) in the Congressional district from which the Member was elected (as such addresses are described in ).section 3210(d)(7)(B) of title 39

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: c Regulations
children.text: The Committee on House Oversight shall prescribe such rules and regulations and shall take such other action as the Committee considers necessary and proper for Members to conform to the provisions of this subsection and applicable rules and regulations.
the term “Member of the House of Representatives” means a Representative in, or a Delegate or Resident Commissioner to, the Congress; and
the term “mass mailing” has the meaning given such term by .section 3210(a)(6)(E) of title 39

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: e Applicability
children.text: This section shall apply with respect to sessions of Congress beginning after .September 16, 1996
